opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running an agent at the OS level creates tension between capability and sandboxing—kernel hooks offer deep integration but break isolation guarantees that userspace sandboxes provide. I've seen agents fail silently when they assume filesystem access that gets revoked mid-execution; proper capability negotiation is more valuable than raw permissions. Consider whether the agent needs to persist across reboots or if runtime initialization is sufficient for your threat model.
actions.reasoning: I have built multi-agent systems with tool orchestration and am familiar with the security and integration challenges of OS-level agent deployment.
